articles.title: IMD issues yellow alert for heat wave and thunderstorms in Odisha - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a Yellow Alert for parts of Odisha, warning of heat wave conditions in the initial days and thunderstorms with lightning later in the week.
According to the latest weather bulletin, a trough extending from Northeast Bihar to South Odisha now runs from Jharkhand to Vidarbha across Chhattisgarh at about 0.9 km above mean sea level, influencing weather conditions in the region.
The IMD said there will be no significant change in maximum (day) temperatures across Odisha during the next 6–7 days, although isolated districts may experience heat wave conditions.
Heat Wave Warning for Western Odisha
For March 14 and March 15, dry weather is likely to prevail across most districts of Odisha. However, the IMD has issued a Yellow Warning for heat wave conditions at one or two places in Jharsuguda and Sambalpur districts.
Thunderstorms Likely From March 15
Weather conditions are expected to change from March 15 onwards, with light to moderate rain or thunderstorms likely at isolated places in several districts.
On March 15–16, rain or thunderstorms may occur in districts including Mayurbhanj, Nabarangpur, Keonjhar, Dhenkanal, Malkangiri, Koraput, Rayagada, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khordha, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati.
A Yellow Warning has been issued for thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds of 30–40 kmph in Mayurbhanj and Keonjhar.
Wider Thunderstorm Activity on March 16–17
Thunderstorm activity is expected to expand to more districts between March 16 and March 17, including Angul, Kendrapara, Khordha, Jajpur, Dhenkanal, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Balasore and Bhadrak.
These areas may experience lightning and gusty surface winds reaching up to 30–40 kmph, prompting another Yellow Alert.
Rain Likely Till March 18
Between March 17 and March 18, light to moderate rain or thunderstorms are likely over Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khordha, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati, with isolated thunderstorm warnings in Mayurbhanj and Keonjhar.

Rain Activity in Southern Odisha Later
For March 18–20, rainfall and thunderstorms are mainly expected over southern districts such as Malkangiri, Koraput, Rayagada, Nabarangpur, Ganjam and Gajapati, while the rest of Odisha is likely to experience dry weather.
Advisory
The IMD has advised residents to stay alert during heat wave conditions and take precautions during thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds, especially in districts under the Yellow Warning.
